content:
When running doctests, is there a cfg that gets defined? Something similar to #[cfg(test)] but for doctests.

I'm writing a driver and its subsystem abstraction. In the abstraction doctests I don't want to call the C API, so I'm mocking the bindings module. At the beginning of my file I'd like to replace the actual bindings with my mock:

// This is the cfg I'm missing
#[cfg(???)]
use crate::bindings_mock as bindings;

It works fine with Rust tests but, as they don't use KUnit, I'm not able to use other kernel stuff.
In case someone else runs into the same issue, #[cfg(CONFIG_KUNIT)] should do the trick: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/950/
